function.name: read_folder

path.description: Path to the folder (can be relative to working directory or absolute)

max_depth.description: Maximum recursion depth when recursive is true. Default: 3

include_hidden.description: Include hidden files (starting with .). Default: false

filter_extensions.description: Only include files with these extensions (e.g., [".js", ".ts"])

function.description: List files and subdirectories in a folder on the local filesystem. Returns metadata including names, paths, types, sizes, and modification times.

function.name: read_file

end_line.description: Read to this line number (1-indexed). Useful for large files.

file_path.description: Path to the file (can be relative to working directory or absolute)

start_line.description: Read from this line number (1-indexed). Useful for large files.

function.description: Read the contents of a text file from the local filesystem. Supports encoding options and line ranges for large files.

function.name: write_file

path.description: Path to the file (can be relative to working directory or absolute)

content.description: The content to write to the file.

overwrite.description: This flags describes whether an existing file will be overwritten. If the file does not exist, this flag has no effect. If the file exists, this flag must be TRUE to over-write the file. If the file exists and this flag is FALSE, the tool function will not write the contents and will return an error. Default is FALSE.

function.description: Create a new file or update an existing file with the specified content. Use overwrite=true to replace existing files.

function.name: windows_shell

command.description: The Windows command to execute. Allowed commands: dir, del, move, rename, type, echo, cd, tree, findstr, where, ver, hostname, ipconfig, ping, tracert, nslookup, systeminfo, tasklist, whoami, git, gh, slack

arguments.description: Arguments to pass to the command (e.g., "/s /b" for dir, "C:" for cd)

function.description: Execute Windows command prompt commands. Only specific whitelisted commands are allowed for security. Returns the command output as a string.

function.name: unix_shell

command.description: The Unix shell command to execute. Allowed commands: ls, cat, echo, pwd, cd, tree, grep, find, which, uname, hostname, ifconfig, ip, ping, traceroute, nslookup, dig, ps, whoami, git, gh, slack

function.description: Execute Unix/Linux/macOS shell commands. Only specific whitelisted commands are allowed for security. Returns the command output as a string.

function.name: read_word

file_path.description: Path to the .docx file (can be relative to working directory or absolute)

function.description: Read the contents of a Microsoft Word (.docx) file and extract plain text. Only supports .docx format (not legacy .doc files).

function.name: read_excel

file_path.description: Path to the .xlsx file (can be relative to working directory or absolute)

sheet_name.description: Optional: Specific sheet name to read. If not provided, all sheets will be read.

function.description: Read the contents of a Microsoft Excel (.xlsx) file and extract data as text. Only supports .xlsx format (not legacy .xls files). Returns data from all sheets in CSV format.

function.name: read_pdf

file_path.description: Path to the .pdf file (can be relative to working directory or absolute)

function.description: Read the contents of a PDF file and extract plain text. Works with standard PDF files.

Here are some of the **hottest trends in 2026** across tech, business, and culture:

### 🤖 1. AI Agents Everywhere
Not just chatbots — autonomous AI agents that:
- Manage emails and schedules  
- Write and review code  
- Run marketing campaigns  
- Automate business workflows  

“Agent-first” software is replacing traditional SaaS in many startups.

---

### 🧠 2. Personal AI (Your AI Twin)
People are training personalized AI models on:
- Their writing style  
- Voice  
- Business knowledge  
- Personal data  

This is huge for creators, executives, and solopreneurs.

---

### 🏢 3. Lean AI Startups
Small teams (2–10 people) building multi‑million dollar companies using:
- AI dev tools  
- Automated operations  
- AI customer support  

Efficiency > headcount is the new flex.

---

### 🔐 4. AI Security & Trust
As AI grows, so does:
- Deepfake detection  
- AI watermarking  
- Model verification  
- Privacy‑first AI  

Security tech is booming.

---

### 🎮 5. AI‑Generated Media
- AI video creation  
- Hyper‑realistic avatars  
- Game worlds generated on the fly  
- Personalized content streams  

Entertainment is becoming interactive and custom.

---

### 🧬 6. Bio + AI
AI is accelerating:
- Drug discovery  
- Longevity research  
- Personalized medicine  
- Wearable health prediction  

Health optimization is becoming mainstream.

---

### 💰 7. AI x Finance
- Algorithmic personal investing  
- AI CFO tools for startups  
- On‑chain + AI integrations  

Automation is reshaping finance workflows.

---

If I had to pick the *biggest* meta‑trend of 2026:

> **AI moving from “assistant” to “autonomous actor.”**

If you'd like, I can tailor trends specifically for:
- Startups
- Investing
- Content creators
- Software developers
- E‑commerce
- Students

What space are you most interested in?
